meta.decision_rationale: Four scenarios explore the library's evolution across 10 years under different cultural/economic/tech drivers. Essential for understanding which governance model protects against which futures.
evidence_refs: Digital library adoption rates in peer libraries
tradeoffs: Detailed scenarios require more facilitation time
Some scenarios may feel pessimistic but are necessary to test governance robustness

scenarios.name: Civic Hub (Preferred)

drivers.description: Community values local institutions; physical gathering spaces appreciated
City budget remains stable; library funding maintained
Digital tools enhance (not replace) physical services; hybrid model thrives

scenarios.description: Library becomes true civic hub: expanded programs, robust collection, active community governance, financial stability.
scenarios.maieutic_question: What governance structure would best enable and protect this scenario?
scenarios.user_response: A transparent, community-inclusive governance model that formalizes input from patrons and staff

scenarios.name: Irrelevance (Feared)

drivers.description: Digital-only services capture most users; physical visits decline steeply
City budget cuts; library funding reduced by 40%
Cultural shift toward private (corporate) gathering spaces

scenarios.description: Library becomes marginal: reduced hours, minimal collection, volunteer-dependent, declining relevance.
scenarios.maieutic_question: What governance guardrails would allow the library to survive and eventually thrive even in this scenario?
scenarios.user_response: Explicit commitment to core values (accessibility, equity, community voice) that survive budget cuts

scenarios.name: Hyperlocal Powerhouse (Wild Card)

drivers.description: Sudden backlash against corporate platforms; local institutions seen as essential
Open-source tools allow libraries to offer services previously locked in corporate platforms
Regional library cooperation creates economies of scale without central control

scenarios.description: Library becomes a node in a federated, community-controlled information network. Unexpected opportunity.
scenarios.maieutic_question: What governance principles would allow us to seize this opportunity while staying accountable to our community?
scenarios.user_response: Governance must be interoperable with other libraries while maintaining local autonomy

scenarios.name: Politicized & Captured (Negative)

drivers.description: New laws give city council direct veto over collection decisions
Library becomes battleground for culture war issues
Funding becomes conditional on compliance with majority political preferences

scenarios.description: Library loses independence; becomes tool of political faction; community loses trust.
scenarios.maieutic_question: What governance structures would protect democratic values and intellectual freedom even under political pressure?
scenarios.user_response: Clear separation between governance (community) and collection decisions; explicit intellectual freedom commitments
